No, adjacency is sharing a side. The cell above $(3,j)$ is $(2,j)$. The cells to the left/right are $(3,j-1)$ and $(3,j+1)$. Since $(3,j)$ is the *first* cell in row 3, he couldn't have come from row 3. He must come from row 2. The only cell in row 2 adjacent to $(3,j)$ is $(2,j)$.
